title: RsaKey::function name is a footgun; magic rsa_type ints are undiscoverable
description: The method name 'function' is vague. The rsa_type parameter takes magic integers 0/1/2/3 (RSA_PRIVATE_ENCRYPT etc.) documented only in a docstring, not as Rust constants. A caller must read the docs, memorize the mapping, and pass a raw i32 with no type safety. This will generate repeated issues asking 'how do I sign with RSA?' Fix: add a Rust enum RsaOperation { PrivateEncrypt=0, PrivateDecrypt=1, PublicEncrypt=2, PublicDecrypt=3 } and change the signature to take it; or at minimum add pub const definitions.
